source: End-to-End Reinforcement Learning for Automatic Taxonomy Induction

concepts.finding: Taxonomy expansion: given existing taxonomy, learn to place new terms into correct parent nodes. RL-based navigation of taxonomy tree for placing new concepts. Implicit edge semantics: edges in taxonomy have semantics (type-of, part-of, related-to) that can be learned from data.
concepts.implication: Keyword-intent taxonomies can be incrementally expanded as new keywords emerge. New keywords can be placed into the taxonomy via RL or nearest neighbor methods.
concepts.open_question: Should keyword-intent taxonomy be static or dynamic (evolving as new keywords/patterns emerge)?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: An automated taxonomy expansion system can place 80%+ of new keywords into correct parent intents with precision > 0.7.
